figures.caption: Figure 5. The two domains of public and private data used for Sections 5.1 and 5.2 (Peng et al., 2019). Both datasets share the same number of classes, with Clipart being a collection of stylized images representing the private data, and Quickdraw representing a collection of hand-draw sketches.

Figure 6. The two domains of public and private data used for Section 5.2 (Peng et al., 2019). Both datasets share the same number of classes, with Infograph being a collection of stylized images representing the public data, and Real representing a collection of real-life images.

Figure 2. Required noise magnitude σ to bound Dα(π T R∥πT L ) as a function of the forget fraction c = nforget/npriv. Values are computed assuming a strongly convex loss (Chien et al., 2024a), for a binary classification task. Details are deferred to Appendix B.3.
Table 3. Class aggregation for experimental dataset. Individual classes are grouped into 24 superclasses.
